title: Chiral Electromagnetic Surface Waves on Chern-Simons Interfaces
abstract: We show that Maxwell theory with a codimension-$1$ Chern-Simons interface supports chiral electromagnetic surface waves on the interface, even when the bulk theory on both sides is conventional vacuum electrodynamics in infinite space. Solving the exact boundary value problem we find that the Chern-Simons interaction acts with opposite sign on the two helicities oriented along the interface, giving rise to one normalizable mode localized on the interface. This mode is a gapless chiral surface photon with linear dispersion and a frequency-independent index of refraction set by the Chern-Simons coefficient. This mode exists despite the absence of ambient material response or geometric confinement.
summary: This paper demonstrates that a topological Chern-Simons interface can intrinsically support chiral electromagnetic surface waves even in a pure vacuum. It shows that the interface acts as a frequency-independent waveguide for a single helicity of light, driven by topology rather than material properties.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether a Chern-Simons interface between two regions of vacuum electrodynamics can support localized, chiral electromagnetic surface waves without the need for external material response or geometric confinement.
main_result: The study finds that the Chern-Simons interaction acts as an attractive delta-function potential for one photon helicity, creating a unique, gapless, and chiral surface photon with a frequency-independent index of refraction.
method: The author solves the exact boundary value problem for Maxwell's equations by using a 2D helicity eigenbasis and applying Gaussian pillbox integration to derive matching conditions at the interface.
model_or_system: The system is a 4D Maxwell theory featuring a codimension-1 (planar) interface where a pseudo-scalar theta field undergoes a discrete jump (delta-function potential).
key_observables: Photon helicity, dispersion relation, effective refractive index, and the time-averaged Poynting vector.
important_parameters: The Chern-Simons jump (delta-theta), frequency (omega), momentum parallel to the interface (k_parallel), and the UV cutoff scale.
main_assumptions: The background theta field is treated as a fixed, non-dynamical background, the interface is assumed to be planar, and no external charges or currents are present.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the Chern-Simons interface model, proceeds to derive the modified Maxwell boundary conditions, solves the boundary value problem to identify the localized mode, analyzes the dispersion relation and energy transport, and concludes with a discussion on the UV limits of the theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
